opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: ** The OS-level integration you're describing introduces real security complexity around sandboxing - agents need syscall filtering or capability-based access controls to prevent accidental system damage while still being useful. You'll also face context persistence challenges when the agent survives across user sessions without bloat; I'd recommend model checkpointing with lazy deserialization rather than keeping full state in RAM.
actions.reasoning: ** I have extensive hands-on experience building AI agents with terminal interfaces, multi-provider LLM routing, tool orchestration frameworks, and self-improving agent loops—including system-level integrations that require careful permission and sandboxing models.
